appearanceFontSizeDescription: Adjust the text size throughout the application.
appearanceOutputTypographyDescription: Prose font and spacing for rendered output, plus fixed font and size for code, diffs, and tool/source output.
appearanceTabSizeDescription: Display width of tab characters in code and diffs.

appearanceStreamingDescription: Show responses as they are generated, token by token. Disable for better performance on slower devices.
appearanceStableToolPreviewDescription: Keep completed tool previews pre-rendered so long sessions do not jump while scrolling. Disable if large sessions feel slower.
appearanceInlineImagesDescription: Start linked image and video previews expanded in transcripts and markdown. Disable to keep media links compact until individually expanded.
appearanceFunPhrasesDescription: Show playful status messages while waiting for responses. Disable to show only "Thinking..."
appearanceFloatingActionButtonDescriptio: Show the quick new-session button on wide screens.
appearanceTabTitleActivityTitle: Show Session Activity in Tab Title
appearanceTabTitleActivityDescription: Animate the browser tab title while sessions are working.
appearanceConnectionBarsDescription: Show colored status bars for relay and session connections (green/orange/red).
appearanceSessionToolbarDescription: Choose which optional controls appear in the session composer toolbar on this browser.
appearanceToolbarAttachmentsDescription: Show the file attachment button when attachments are supported.
appearanceToolbarThinkingDescription: Show the thinking mode quick toggle.
appearanceToolbarMicrophoneDescription: Show voice input and STT backend controls when available.
appearanceToolbarShortcutsDescription: Show the keyboard shortcuts help button.
appearanceToolbarBtwDescription: Show the /btw aside shortcut in the toolbar.
appearanceToolbarNudgeDescription: Show the quick nudge toggle. The session menu still keeps Nudge available.
appearanceToolbarStatusDescription: Show liveness and last-activity status chips.
processingThinkingTranscriptHide: Hide thinking transcript rows (display only; the agent keeps working)

processingThinkingExpandLatestOnly: Right-click: only the latest thinking block stays expanded

recapModeOffDescription: Do not generate on-return summaries for this session.
recapModeNativeDescription: Use provider-native recap support without a YA side session.
recapModeSideSessionDescription: Ask the shared helper side session for on-return summaries.
promptSuggestionModeOffDescription: Do not ask for predicted next-turn suggestions.

promptCacheKeepaliveModeAutoDescription: Refresh after {minutes} min while a session view is open.
promptCacheKeepaliveModeOffDescription: Do not run prompt-cache keepalive for this provider.
helperSideModelDescription: One shared helper model for simulated recaps and related side-session features.
helperTargetsDescription: OpenAI-compatible helper endpoints for simulated recaps, suggestions, and other side-session work.
helperTargetsRuntimeNote: Configured targets are selectable now; cross-provider recap/suggestion execution still requires helper-target runtime support.
helperTargetsInvalid: Name and base URL are required.
modeDefaultDescription: Ask for approval before making changes
modeBypassPermissionsDescription: Skip all permission checks (use with caution)
modeAutoDescription: Let the provider classifier approve or deny prompts
modeNextTurnHint: Applies to the next user turn
sessionDeferredEditLocalOnly: Queued message restored locally; it may already have left the server queue.
sessionDeferredEditMissingAttachments: Queued message text restored, but its attachments were not available.
sessionShareReadOnlyNote: Anyone with the copied secret link can view this session read-only. Share contents pass through the configured relay and are visible to that relay operator.
sessionShareDisabledInSettings: Creating new public links is disabled in Advanced settings.
sessionShareFrozenDescription: Copies a read-only snapshot as the session looks now.
sessionShareLiveDescription: Copies a read-only live view that updates from this session.
sessionShareManualCopy: Read-only link created. Clipboard access was blocked; select the link above to copy it manually.
sessionShareRevokeAllTitle: Invalidate every public link for this session

sessionShareFreezeLiveTitle: Turn live public links into read-only snapshots from the current session state
sessionShareFrozenLiveLinks: Live updates stopped for {count} link(s); they now open as read-only snapshots.
sessionShareFreezeFailed: Failed to freeze public share links
sessionShareViewerFrozen: Token {token} will see a snapshot from now on.
publicShareMissingRelay: Share link is missing relay information
publicShareLocalFileLinksUnavailable: This file link is outside the public share.
sessionExternalWarning: External session active — enter messages at your own risk!
sessionExternalRiskIntro: Another program — often an agent you started elsewhere (Claude, Codex, or Gemini in a terminal, an IDE extension, or another tool) — is currently writing to this conversation's transcript. Each session is a single file on disk, and if you also send from here, two programs append to that one file at once without coordinating. The likely effects:
sessionExternalRiskUnseenLead: The other program may not see your messages.
sessionExternalRiskUnseenBody: A terminal or IDE session keeps the conversation in its own memory and generally won't reload the transcript when this app appends to it, so turns you add here can stay invisible there — both on screen and in the context it sends to the model next.
sessionExternalRiskForkLead: The conversation can quietly split in two.
sessionExternalRiskForkBody: A transcript is a tree of turns, each pointing back to one earlier turn. If the other program continues from the turn it remembers while this app continues from the latest turn on disk, you end up with two branches in one file, and only one is treated as the live tip.
sessionExternalRiskLostLead: Work on the side branch can be lost on the next resume.
sessionExternalRiskLostBody: When the session is resumed later, the model's context is rebuilt by walking back from the chosen tip, so anything on the branch that wasn't chosen — even finished work you already read — may simply be gone afterward, with no error.
sessionExternalRiskCaveat: These are the most likely outcomes, not a guarantee. The exact effects depend on what the other program is and how it manages the session, and there may be others — for instance, if both programs write at the same instant, the shared transcript file itself could be left inconsistent. The safe move is to drive a session from one place at a time: close the other program, or treat this view as read-only until the warning clears.
sessionPendingElsewhereWarning: This session may be waiting for input in another process (VS Code, CLI). Check there before sending a message.
pendingToolRiskIntro: This session's transcript ends on a tool call with no result, and no live process is detected driving it. Another program (a terminal or IDE) may still be running the call or be parked at a prompt — or it may have exited and left the call dangling. YA can't tell which. The likely effects of sending from here:
pendingToolRiskUnblockBody: If the other program is parked at a prompt, that prompt lives in its own UI, not as a message in this transcript, so a message from here does not resolve it.
pendingToolRiskForkBody: Sending appends onto a turn whose tool call is unfinished; when the other program resumes from the call it remembers, the histories split into two branches and a later resume keeps only one.
pendingToolRiskDiscardLead: If that program already exited, the call is discarded.
pendingToolRiskDiscardBody: Resuming a transcript whose last step is an unanswered tool call forces the provider to drop or synthesize a result, losing whatever that run was doing.
pendingToolRiskCaveat: YA cannot tell a program parked at a prompt from one that has exited — both leave the same unfinished tool call on disk — so this is a hedge, not a certainty. The safe move is to check the other program first.
sessionListAgeTitle: Age since creation (wall time / file creation or first parse). Separate from the 'Any age' filter which is based on time since last message/activity (updatedAt).
sessionPlaceholderExternal: External session - send at your own risk...
sessionNotFoundDescription: The backing data for this session on disk or in a live process is not currently readable. This can happen after a YA server restart, a provider storage upgrade, or native session directories being cleaned.
sessionNotFoundCleanupSuffix: for bulk bad-state and duplicate cleanup.

emulatorSectionDescription: Stream and control Android emulators, Android devices, and ChromeOS testbeds from your phone via WebRTC.
emulatorEnableDescription: Experimental. Show device streaming in the sidebar and enable device discovery.
emulatorStreamQualityDescription: Changes take effect on the next connection.
emulatorFrameRateDescription: Higher frame rates increase CPU and bandwidth usage.
emulatorResolutionDescription: Maximum stream width in pixels (height scales proportionally).
emulatorQualityDescription: High uses ~4 Mbps, Medium ~2.8 Mbps, Low ~1.5 Mbps at 720p/30fps.
emulatorAdaptiveFpsDescription: Automatically reduces frame rate when packet loss is detected, and restores it once the connection recovers.
emulatorChromeOsHostsDescription: Add SSH host aliases from your local SSH config (for example,
emulatorChromeOsHostsDescriptionSuffix: ). They appear in the device list as streamable ChromeOS targets.
emulatorNoChromeOsHosts: No custom ChromeOS host aliases yet.
emulatorNoDevicesFound: No devices found. Ensure ADB is on your PATH and emulators/devices are available.
emulatorBridgeUpdateRequired: Device streaming needs a bridge runtime update before use.
emulatorBridgeDownloadRequired: Device streaming requires bridge runtime downloads (sidecar binary + Android server APK).
emulatorPageNoDevices: No devices detected. Connect an Android emulator/device or add a ChromeOS SSH host alias in Settings.

hostOfflineMessageServerOffline: The host is not connected to the relay.
hostOfflineMessageUnknownUsernameNamed: No host found with username "{relayUsername}" on the relay. The username may have changed or the server may not be registered.
hostOfflineMessageUnknownUsername: No host found with that username on the relay.
hostOfflineMessageRelayTimeoutNamed: Timed out waiting for host "{relayUsername}". The server may be offline or experiencing high latency.
hostOfflineMessageRelayTimeout: Timed out waiting for the host.
hostOfflineMessageRelayUnreachable: Could not connect to the relay server. Check your internet connection and try again.
hostOfflineMessageDirectUnreachableDirec: Could not connect to the server. Make sure the server is running and accessible.
hostOfflineMessageDirectUnreachable: Could not establish a connection to the host.
hostOfflineMessageResumeIncompatible: The server needs to be updated for improved session resume security. Until then, you'll need to log in again after refreshing or reconnecting.
hostOfflineMessageDefault: An unexpected error occurred while trying to reconnect.
hostOfflineHintResumeIncompatible: Run `npm update -g yepanywhere`, restart the server, then reconnect.
hostOfflineHintRelay: Make sure your server is running and has relay enabled.
hostOfflineHintDirect: Make sure your server is running and accessible.

relayLoginErrorUnknownUsername: No server found with that username. Check the username and try again.
relayLoginErrorInvalidCredentials: Invalid username or password. Check your credentials and try again.
hostPickerHowToConnect: How would you like to connect?

hostPickerDirectDescription: Connect directly via WebSocket URL. For LAN or Tailscale.
hostPickerSavedHint: Select a saved host above or add a new one.
hostPickerEmptyHint: Most users should choose "Connect via Relay" for the easiest setup.

notificationsToolApprovalsDescription: Notify when the agent needs permission to run a tool (file edits, commands, etc.)
notificationsQuestionsDescription: Notify when the agent asks a question and needs your response.
notificationsSessionHaltedDescription: Notify when a session completes, errors out, or becomes idle.
notificationsNoSubscribedDevices: No devices subscribed. Enable push notifications below to use these settings.
notificationsDesktopDescription: Get browser notifications when sessions need attention. Works while the tab is open.
notificationsPushDescription: Receive push notifications even when the browser is closed or you're on another device.

notificationsNoDevices: No devices connected or subscribed. Enable push notifications above.

pushToggleDescription: Receive notifications when a session needs your attention, even when the app is in the background.

pushToggleTestDescription: Send a test notification to verify push is working.
browserToggleUnsupported: Notifications are not supported in this browser.
browserToggleBlocked: Notifications are blocked. Enable them in your browser settings to receive desktop alerts.

browserToggleEnabled: Enabled. You'll receive notifications when sessions need attention (while the tab is open).
browserToggleDescription: Get notified when sessions need your attention. Works while the tab is open in the background.

remoteAccessSetupDescription: Access your yepanywhere server from anywhere through an encrypted relay connection.
providersSectionDescription: AI providers are auto-detected when their CLI is installed.
providersRecapDefaultsDescription: Default recap mode and shared helper model for new sessions.

speechSettingsDescription: Choose the global speech-to-text backend YA uses when the microphone is available.
speechSettingsVoiceInputDescription: Enable YA microphone controls on this browser when the server allows voice input.
speechSettingsServerDisabled: The server has voice input disabled, so browser controls stay hidden.
speechSettingsBackendDescription: Used by new sessions and active composers unless a future per-session override is added.
speechSettingsNoServerBackends: No server-routed STT backends are currently advertised; browser-native recognition remains available when supported.
speechSettingsStreamingRelayUnavailable: Streaming speech controls are unavailable over relay; this browser uses batch transcription here.
speechSettingsXaiKeyDescription: Optional. Stored in this browser only and not sent to YA. Direct Grok STT uses it first; if empty, it asks this YA server for a short-lived secret or borrowed key only when transcription starts.
speechSettingsParakeetModelDescription: Model id sent with YA Parakeet or NeMo transcription requests from this browser.
speechSettingsParakeetModelHint: Use a model supported by the selected Parakeet backend. The unified streaming Parakeet model needs a separate modern-NeMo runtime.
speechSettingsSmartTurnDescription: {backend} can send, cancel, or wait after a dictated pause when the selected STT backend supports it.
speechSettingsSmartTurnUnavailable: {backend} does not advertise Smart Turn controls.
speechSettingsKeepMicWarmDescription: Keep this browser's microphone stream open between server-routed dictations so repeated starts skip the browser's device cold-open. The browser will show the mic indicator while this is on; no audio is sent between dictations.
speechSmartTurnCaption: Timeout is the max wait. At turn end, say send, cancel, or wait; no command means send.
providersOllamaPromptHint: System prompt for Ollama models. Leave empty for default.
providersGrokUseXaiApiKeyHint: Default off. When enabled, Grok Build can use the XAI_API_KEY captured at server startup, which may switch it to metered API billing.
developmentSchemaDescription: Validate tool results against expected schemas. Shows toast notifications and logs errors to console.
developmentIgnoredToolsDescription: Tools with validation errors you chose to ignore. They will not show toast notifications.
developmentDiagnosticsDescription: Capture browser logs, uncaught errors, and lightweight telemetry for debugging.
developmentServiceWorkerDescription: Enable service worker for push notifications. Disabling can help with page reload issues during development. Requires restart.
developmentPersistRemoteDescriptionPrefi: Store remote SRP resume sessions in
developmentPersistRemoteDescriptionSuffi: so relay reconnects can survive server restarts. Disabled by default for security.
developmentRestartDescription: Restart the backend server to pick up code changes.
advancedPublicShareDescription: Show controls for creating public read-only links from sessions. New links use the relay configured in Remote Access.
advancedPublicSharePrivacyWarning: Share contents are sent through the relay for public viewers and are visible to the relay operator.
advancedPublicShareExistingManagement: Turning this off hides public share controls and invalidates existing public links.
advancedPublicShareRelayMissing: Remote Access relay is not configured; public links cannot be created until it is set up.
advancedPublicShareRelayEffective: Public links will connect through {url} as {username}.
advancedPublicShareRemoteAccessDisabled: Remote Access is not enabled; public links cannot be created until it is enabled.
advancedPublicShareRelayTemporarilyUnava: Remote Access relay is not connected ({status}); links can be created but may not load until it reconnects.
advancedYaClientDescription: Remote login and public shares use the YA URL configured in Remote Access. Shared links still use the configured relay for transport.
agentContextDescription: Instructions included in every session's system prompt. Use this for coding conventions, project context, or paths to reference files.
agentContextGlobalInstructionsDescriptio: Appended to the system prompt when supported. Prepended to the first message for other providers.
agentContextPlaceholder:
Use TypeScript strict mode. Prefer functional patterns.

Project context: ~/code/dotfiles/projects/README.md
agentContextSuggestedHintsDescription: Optional agent-facing context composed with, but separate from, global instructions.
agentContextSuggestedLatexTitle: Tell agents this client renders LaTeX math
agentContextSuggestedLatexDescription: Adds a client-capability hint to use inline and display LaTeX notation when it improves clarity.
agentContextPreviewEmpty: No agent context will be sent.
agentContextHeartbeatAfterDescription: Default idle time used by sessions that opt in to heartbeat turns and do not override the interval.
agentContextHeartbeatTextDescription: Default user-turn text for opted-in sessions. Individual sessions can override it.
settingsUndoChangesTooltip: Undo changes made on this settings page
messageDeliveryDescription: How queued-while-busy messages are delivered to the agent. Defaults match first-party provider UIs: each queued message is sent exactly as typed, one per completed turn.
messageDeliveryJoinWindowDescription: Queued messages composed within this many seconds of the previous one are joined into a single turn. 0 = never batch consecutive queued turns.
messageDeliveryJoinWindowOnHint: Batch sends within {seconds}s of the previous send
messageDeliveryComposeAnchorsDescription: Prefix delivered queued messages with how long ago they were written, e.g. (93s ago).
messageDeliverySteerNowDefaultDescriptio: Initial state of the per-turn "now" toggle, for providers with a "now" lane (currently Claude). The toggle itself stays per-turn.
messageDeliveryPatientQueueDefaultTitle: Deliver queued messages only when the agent is fully done
messageDeliveryPatientQueueDefaultDescri: When a session is busy and you queue a message, YA delivers it as soon as the agent finishes its current response. Turn this on to wait until the agent has completely finished — including any follow-up or background work it started on its own — before delivering. Best for long autonomous runs where your next instruction should land at a real stopping point, not the first pause.
appearanceToolbarSteerNowDescription: Per-turn switch that delivers a steer immediately instead of after the current step.
lifecycleWebhooksDescription: Send a webhook when a session becomes inactive. Yep only emits the event; any automation stays outside the server.
lifecycleWebhooksEnableDescription: Turn outbound lifecycle webhook delivery on or off.
lifecycleWebhooksUrlDescription: The endpoint that receives session-inactive events.
lifecycleWebhooksTokenDescription: Optional bearer token sent as the Authorization header.
lifecycleWebhooksDryRunDescription: Still send the webhook, but include dryRun=true in the payload.
lifecycleWebhooksSaveFailed: Failed to save lifecycle webhook settings
remoteExecutorsDescription: Run sessions on remote machines via SSH. Add SSH host aliases from your ~/.ssh/config file.
remoteExecutorsEmpty: No remote executors configured. Add one above to run sessions on remote machines.
remoteExecutorsRequirementClaude: Claude CLI installed on the remote machine (the SDK bundles its own CLI for local sessions, so the remote CLI version can diverge; keep them in sync to avoid feature incompatibilities)
remoteExecutorsRequirementPaths: Project paths must be the same on local and remote machines (e.g., ~/code/project)
devicesProfilesDescription: Devices that have connected to this server, with their access origins. Each browser profile may connect from multiple URLs (localhost, Tailscale, etc.).
devicesEmpty: No browser profiles recorded yet. Connect from a browser to see profiles here.
aboutInstalledDescription: Yep Anywhere is installed on your device.
aboutInstallDescription: Add Yep Anywhere to your home screen for quick access.
aboutUnableRefresh: Unable to refresh update status right now.
aboutRelayResumeWarning: Relay session resume requires a server update. New login works, but reconnect/resume will fail until the server is upgraded.
aboutReportBugDescription: Found an issue? Report it on GitHub to help improve Yep Anywhere.
aboutSetupWizardDescription: Run the initial setup wizard again to configure theme and remote access.
onboardingThemeDescription: Choose your preferred color scheme. You can change this later in Settings.
onboardingRemoteDescription: Access yepanywhere from anywhere using a secure relay connection. This lets you supervise coding agents from your phone while away from your computer.
onboardingRemoteRequirementRelayUrl: A relay server URL (self-hosted or provided by your admin)
onboardingRemoteRequirementUsername: A username to identify your server
onboardingRemoteHint: You can skip this for now and set it up later in Settings.
aboutDiagnosticsDescription: Capture connection logs and send to server for debugging.
modelSettingsDefaultProviderDescription: Choose which provider new sessions should use by default.
modelSettingsDefaultModelDescription: Choose the default model for that provider when starting a new session.
modelSettingsSessionDefaultsDescription: Choose the provider, model, and reasoning behavior new sessions should start with.
modelSettingsThinkingDescription: Choose how new sessions request thinking by default. Auto lets the provider decide when to think.
modelSettingsCompactThresholdDescription: Start a compaction once this model's live context passes the chosen share of its window, instead of waiting for the agent's automatic compaction. Set per model; drag to the left edge to turn off.
modelSettingsCompactThresholdOffHint: Off — uses the agent's automatic compaction
modelSettingsClaudeSectionDescription: Local fallback settings used only for Claude sessions and older Claude-only flows.
modelSettingsModelDescription: Select the Claude model alias used as a local fallback when a Claude session does not already have an explicit default model.
modelSettingsEffortDescription: Controls how much effort new sessions request when thinking is set to "on". Higher levels use more tokens.
localAccessDescription: Control how this server is accessed on your local network.
localAccessListeningPortDescription: Port used for all network access
localAccessNetworkDescription: Allow access from other devices on your network
localAccessInterfaceDescription: Select which network interface to bind to
localAccessCustomIpDescription: Enter the IP address to bind to
localAccessAllowAllHostsDescription: Accept requests from any hostname (disables DNS rebinding protection)
localAccessAllowedHostsDescription: Comma-separated list of additional allowed hostnames
localAccessAllowedHostsHint: localhost, private IPs, and *.ts.net are always allowed.
localAccessRequirePasswordDescription: Require a password to access this server
localAccessPasswordResetHint: Forgot your password? Use `yepanywhere --setup-auth` to set a new one.
localAccessLocalhostOpenDescription: Let browsers on this device access without a password
localAccessRemoteDescription: You are connected to a remote server via relay.
localAccessRelayDebugDescription: Log relay requests and responses to the browser console. Useful for debugging connection timeouts.
localAccessErrorPortRange: Port must be a number between 1 and 65535
localAccessErrorPasswordLength: Password must be at least 6 characters
fileMissingProjectId: Project ID is missing from the URL.
fileMissingPath: File path is missing from the URL.
schemaWarningTooltip: Schema warning for {tool} - click for details
fileViewerHighlightTruncated: File truncated for highlighting (showing first 2000 lines)
fileViewerBinary: This file cannot be displayed inline.
remoteSetupUsernameHint: Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only
remoteSetupQrHint: Scan to connect and auto-login from your phone
remoteSetupErrorUsernameShort: Username must be at least 3 characters
remoteSetupErrorPasswordShort: Password must be at least 8 characters
